VUB–Leeds Algebra School (2026)

8–12 June 2026 • Brussels, Belgium

Organised within the topYB International Joint Research Group (IJRG).

TopYB | Overview | Registration | Poster session | Programme | Location & Travel | Organisers

Overview

A training week for MSc students, PhD students, postdocs, and researchers, featuring four mini-courses and a poster session.

4 mini-courses Poster session Networking
Registration View draft programme

Poster

Download printable poster (PDF)

Key information

Dates
8–12 June 2026.
City
Brussels, Belgium.
Venue
LIC.0.04 Learning Theatre - Etterbeek Campus.
Audience
MSc / PhD / Postdoc / Researchers.
Format
Four mini-courses + poster session.
Fees
Registration is now open. It is free for participants affiliated with VUB and EUR 50 for others. The fee will be used to partially cover coffee breaks and lunches.

Mini-courses

Combinatorial surface models for cluster algebras and cluster categories

Instructor: Karin Baur
(Ruhr-Universität Bochum & University of Leeds)

Symmetric tensor categories

Instructor: Pavel Etingof
(Massachusetts Institute of Technology)

Algebraic and categorical structures for modular functors and quantum codes

Instructor: Christoph Schweigert
(Hamburg University)

Homology of racks and quandles

Instructor: Markus Szymik
(University of Sheffield)

Deadlines

  • Registration: May 15.
  • Poster abstract submission: April 15.
  • Notification: April 15.

Funding

Unfortunately, we cannot provide financial support for participants.

Code of conduct

We are committed to providing a welcoming, respectful, and inclusive environment for all participants, regardless of background or identity. Please treat others with courtesy and professionalism, and respect different viewpoints and experiences. Harassment, discrimination, intimidation, or disruptive behavior are not tolerated.

If you experience or witness a concern, please contact the organisers (details on the Organisers page). Issues will be handled promptly and, when necessary, participants may be asked to leave the event.

Questions?

Contact: Leandro Vendramin (VUB) • Ilaria Colazzo (Leeds)

Sponsors

Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB) • University of Leeds • De Gruyter Brill

© 2026 VUB–Leeds Algebra Summer School
